docs.title: ContextGo Docs
docs.description: Customer-facing documentation for setup, agents, hooks, scheduled tasks, skill market, remote access, and release operations.
docs.featuredDescription: ContextGo docs split into three layers: Guides explain how to start, Features explain what the product can do, and Operations explain how remote access, cloud identity, and release workflows actually work.

categories.title: Guides
categories.description: Start here to understand the product model, install flow, and how users should approach the first working setup.

categories.title: Features
categories.description: These docs explain the functional surfaces users actually operate: agents, hooks, schedules, runtimes, connectors, and the skill market.

categories.title: Operations
categories.description: These docs define cloud account behavior, remote-access mechanics, release operations, and troubleshooting guidance.

entries.title: Set up ContextGo on your first desktop
entries.summary: Install the desktop app, sign in, bind the current device, and confirm the local host is ready for mobile and browser access.

entries.title: Understand the ContextGo product model before you scale usage
entries.summary: Clarify the relationship between desktop host, mobile shell, cloud account, WebUI, and the release repository before onboarding more devices or teammates.

entries.title: Agent entry, assistants, and the working model inside ContextGo
entries.summary: Understand what the agent entry is for, how assistant management differs from agent entry, and why those concepts should not be collapsed into one vague settings bucket.

entries.title: Harness mode and agent collaboration workflows
entries.summary: Explain how local harness mode coexists with remote workflows, and why agent collaboration is a first-class working style rather than an implementation detail.

entries.title: Use hooks to extend workflows at the right moment
entries.summary: Hooks are the product mechanism for inserting controlled behavior around execution events. Users need a clear trigger model, not just a configuration form.

entries.title: Scheduled tasks and cron-driven automation
entries.summary: Explain how periodic execution works, what kinds of automation belong in scheduled tasks, and what users should watch for in long-running operations.

entries.title: Skill Market and reusable capability packages
entries.summary: Show users how the skill market expands what ContextGo can do, how to think about local versus downloadable skills, and how installation should stay trustworthy.

entries.title: Manage local runtimes and CLI health
entries.summary: Install supported runtimes, repair broken CLI environments, and understand why a runtime can be installed but still not be usable yet.

entries.title: Connectors, channels, and publishing paths
entries.summary: Explain how sources and channels fit into ContextGo, and why connectors are part of the product core rather than optional integrations.

entries.title: How remote access works in ContextGo
entries.summary: Understand the desktop-host model, why remote access depends on a stable device-side connection, and what users should expect on mobile.

entries.title: What the ContextGo cloud account syncs
entries.summary: Clarify what the cloud layer is for, what gets linked to the account, and why it should not be confused with full cloud execution.

entries.title: Updates, releases, and common troubleshooting paths
entries.summary: Document how in-app updates relate to the website download center and release repository, and give users a single operational troubleshooting entry point.
